raw_response: {"rating": 6.5, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "This is a news report. The author’s own framing repeatedly adopts pro-life/right-leaning terminology and perspectives while platforming only anti-abortion sources, portraying abortion-rights measures and backers negatively. The consistency and density of one-sided language and sourcing warrant a Right rating (+6.5). Detailed Analysis: (1) Loaded labeling in author voice: uses \"pro-abortion amendments,\" \"pro-abortion organizations,\" and describes Ohio as codifying the \"supposed right to abortion,\" signaling rejection of abortion-rights framing; (2) Moralizing diction favoring the pro-life perspective: refers to \"unborn babies\" and characterizes the 2022 Montana measure as about \"babies born alive in botched abortions\"; (3) Adopts advocacy descriptors without attributional distance: writes that GOP leaders campaigned against \"radical measures\" and credits them in the author’s own prose, rather than as claims; (4) Negative portrayal of the opposing side’s motives and funding in author voice: says measures are backed by \"left-wing organizations\" and \"out-of-state dark money groups,\" and by \"billionaires with eugenicist leanings,\" and that they \"massively\" outspend pro-life groups; (5) Strategic framing against ballot measures: calls them an \"offensive weapon\" and \"basically irreversible,\" warning of their precedence over legislatures with no balancing counterpoint; (6) One-sided source selection: only quotes or paraphrases pro-life organizations (Heartbeat International, Live Action, SBA Pro-Life America) and offers no abortion-rights advocates or neutral experts; (7) Editorial choices reinforce direction: headline centers pro-life optimism about defeats, and a \"READ MORE\" link highlights a \"Left-Wing Coalition\" spending large sums, further casting the abortion-rights side negatively; (8) Omission: lacks substantive explanation from abortion-rights proponents about the failed measures or responses to the funding and motive claims, leaving a singular pro-life narrative."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report. The author’s own framing repeatedly adopts pro-life/right-leaning terminology and perspectives while platforming only anti-abortion sources, portraying abortion-rights measures and backers negatively. The consistency and density of one-sided language and sourcing warrant a Right rating (+6.5). Detailed Analysis: (1) Loaded labeling in author voice: uses "pro-abortion amendments," "pro-abortion organizations," and describes Ohio as codifying the "supposed right to abortion," signaling rejection of abortion-rights framing; (2) Moralizing diction favoring the pro-life perspective: refers to "unborn babies" and characterizes the 2022 Montana measure as about "babies born alive in botched abortions"; (3) Adopts advocacy descriptors without attributional distance: writes that GOP leaders campaigned against "radical measures" and credits them in the author’s own prose, rather than as claims; (4) Negative portrayal of the opposing side’s motives and funding in author voice: says measures are backed by "left-wing organizations" and "out-of-state dark money groups," and by "billionaires with eugenicist leanings," and that they "massively" outspend pro-life groups; (5) Strategic framing against ballot measures: calls them an "offensive weapon" and "basically irreversible," warning of their precedence over legislatures with no balancing counterpoint; (6) One-sided source selection: only quotes or paraphrases pro-life organizations (Heartbeat International, Live Action, SBA Pro-Life America) and offers no abortion-rights advocates or neutral experts; (7) Editorial choices reinforce direction: headline centers pro-life optimism about defeats, and a "READ MORE" link highlights a "Left-Wing Coalition" spending large sums, further casting the abortion-rights side negatively; (8) Omission: lacks substantive explanation from abortion-rights proponents about the failed measures or responses to the funding and motive claims, leaving a singular pro-life narrative.

explanation: This is a news report. The author’s own framing repeatedly adopts pro-life/right-leaning terminology and perspectives while platforming only anti-abortion sources, portraying abortion-rights measures and backers negatively. The consistency and density of one-sided language and sourcing warrant a Right rating (+6.5). Detailed Analysis: (1) Loaded labeling in author voice: uses "pro-abortion amendments," "pro-abortion organizations," and describes Ohio as codifying the "supposed right to abortion," signaling rejection of abortion-rights framing; (2) Moralizing diction favoring the pro-life perspective: refers to "unborn babies" and characterizes the 2022 Montana measure as about "babies born alive in botched abortions"; (3) Adopts advocacy descriptors without attributional distance: writes that GOP leaders campaigned against "radical measures" and credits them in the author’s own prose, rather than as claims; (4) Negative portrayal of the opposing side’s motives and funding in author voice: says measures are backed by "left-wing organizations" and "out-of-state dark money groups," and by "billionaires with eugenicist leanings," and that they "massively" outspend pro-life groups; (5) Strategic framing against ballot measures: calls them an "offensive weapon" and "basically irreversible," warning of their precedence over legislatures with no balancing counterpoint; (6) One-sided source selection: only quotes or paraphrases pro-life organizations (Heartbeat International, Live Action, SBA Pro-Life America) and offers no abortion-rights advocates or neutral experts; (7) Editorial choices reinforce direction: headline centers pro-life optimism about defeats, and a "READ MORE" link highlights a "Left-Wing Coalition" spending large sums, further casting the abortion-rights side negatively; (8) Omission: lacks substantive explanation from abortion-rights proponents about the failed measures or responses to the funding and motive claims, leaving a singular pro-life narrative.
